strengths: Maps directly onto every element named in the seed one-liner (apprenticeship, materials, exhibition, instrument-of-the-month, journal, workshop directory)
Motif is exceptionally concrete: frontispiece, acanthus cartouche, MDCCXIV roundel, tipped-in plate, wax-seal nav, CAP. III chapter headers
Layout patterns are richly specified per page (two-column justified, drop caps, ligatures, foliate rules, copperplate captions)
Interactions are domain-specific and useful (wax-seal hover, maple-billet rotator quarter/flat-sawn, footnote pop-overs, page-turn transition)
Palette mapping is precise with hex values reserved for chrome, ground, and rules
Seven pages, well-differentiated, none feel like filler
weaknesses: Risk of veering into pastiche if engravings are weak; relies heavily on illustration quality
Two-column justified body with drop caps is technically demanding to render cleanly at all breakpoints

strengths: Fresh angle on heritage as a living working network rather than a museum
Motif of ledger ruling, edge tabs, rubber-stamp progress marks, marginalia is original and concrete
Commission ledger and on-the-bench pages give genuinely useful interactive utility
Strong content models with real fields (folio number, lot number, lead time, seats remaining)
weaknesses: Drops the seed's explicit 'annual exhibition gallery' page entirely, weakening alignment
Renames apprenticeship into a courses page, slightly diluting the seed's apprenticeship-program emphasis
Less ornate than the seed's 'ornate editorial heritage' visual feel calls for; feels more workshop than guild-frontispiece
Typewriter-serif body and ledger ruling could read as bookkeeping rather than the lush walnut/parchment register
